(ref. KIRBY) (prod. Paul McCartney)

OG Filename: KW - Only One Ref (4.10.14)

"As a fan of him, I wrote what I believe what someone would say if they love him. I had to be careful to not include things that were said in tabloids. I had to empathize. This was about a man. It wasn’t just about Kanye. You have to not think about the fact you’re working with a celebrity."

Pharrell's verse originates from a freestyle on Rap City in 2006, and Kanye would perform his verse live at Hot 97 Summer Jam 2008, which is said by Designer to originate from an OG version of "Power" that has yet to leak. Original snippet from MusicMafia leaked in 2018, with the same snippet leaking tagless on December 2nd, 2024. Another snippet would be previewed in VC on Auger96's stream July 24th, 2025 during an attempted groupbuy for the song, before a tagged full version would ultimately forceleak July 26th, 2025 with the untagged leaking within the hour.

Name:
⭐ Mama's Boy [V11]
(prod. Madlib & Jeff Bhasker)
(Mama's Boyfriend, My Momma's Boyfriend)
Notes:
OG Filename: Mama's Boy_JBAug2010
Track 10 on the original My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy tracklist shared by Complex. Listed as bonus track by HipHopDX under name "Momma's Boyfriend". Initially leaked June 13th, 2011, months after the album released. Kanye's team released a statement stating the beat was fake, but it was played during a Q-Tip Red Bull Interview 5 years later. Madlib, who likely did one of the sample chops, implied Kanye didn't want to pay him for his work and possibly wanted to take primary credit for the beat, which might have been why the song was scrapped. Samples "The Velvet Swing" by Acoustic Alchemy and "Heritage of a Black Man" by Sam Dees. OG File surfaced May 28th, 2025.

Name:
✨ Ty Dolla $ign - Woke Up
(feat. Kanye West) (prod. Charlie Heat)
Notes: Originally thought to have been a finished SHMG throwaway, but the naming of the original German filename means it was probably given to Ty$, similar to "Guard Down." Has a minute of rapping and singing vocals from Kanye, as well as punch-ins and verses from Ty Dolla over the initial reference track. Samples "Paradise" by Jeremih. Mistaken as a LOVE EVERYONE era track, since the producer for "Paradise" revealed Kanye had reached out Christmas 2016 for the stems - later using them to make an altered version of the song to use as background for a sentimental home video posted by Kim Kardashian to twitter. Also thought to be made later because "Paradise" wasn't released until December 2015, but it was found that the song was previewed before, with Jeremih's album having already been delayed multiple times since 2013. Original snippet leaked April 17th, 2024. Leaked after a successful Soakbuy.

Name:
Fade [V8]
(feat. Post Malone & Ty Dolla $ign) (prod. Kanye West, Benji B, Charlie Handsome, Anthony Kilhoffer & MIKE DEAN)
Notes: Earliest known version with Post Malone, recorded after Kylie Jenner's eighteenth birthday party. Post Malone freestyles for around 16 minutes, and his vocals used in the released version are taken from this freestyle. Post Malone stated that "[we] went in the studio...we just recorded the scratch vocals and then I wrote over it". Same intro, chorus, bridge and first verse as the released version but in the part where Kanye says "I love to... I wanna... I'm tryna..." on the final version he is mumbling. Original snippet leaked November 7th, 2020.

Post Malone has said that "Kanye produced it and did the melody and shit. Had the whole idea for it, it was called 'Instalove'". Original snippet leaked on November 7th, 2020.

Being of similar BPM, similar key and even similar tracklist placement, many speculated that these songs were somehow connected. It turns out that the idea for "Moon" originates from Don Toliver's "Hurricane" reference track. This is likely one of the first versions of the song, with Evan using vocals from the reference to create a new idea.

Features punch-ins from Dem Jointz meant as reference vocals for Kanye. Incorrectly believed to be produced by Pharrell Williams due to the 4-count intro. Samples "African King Ensemble" by Mike Murray, taken from the version used in Logic Pro's loop library.

Name:
✨ Forever [V3]
(prod. Keyon Christ)
Notes: Originally thought to have been the outro for "Can U Be" / "Pressure" (it's unrelated entirely). Likely from early 2015 as Kanye was working closely with Rihanna & Keyon Christ on her album, as well as it being ripped from the same CD as "Pick Up Your Speed" - possibly a CD of references for Rihanna - when sent to Christina Aguilera. There are acapella & instrumental files from July 2015, but those were likely bounced to send to Darren King and other producers. The song title was confirmed when Keyon Christ referred to the snippet that leaked with Kanye vox as "#Forever". Keyon later released a version of the instrumental on SoundCloud.

Glory [V3]
(feat. Dr. Dre & Dem Jointz) (prod. Dem Jointz & Dr. Dre)

Notes:
OG Filename: Glory - snippet v3 extended vocal tape stop
Version with only the Dr. Dre feature. Dem Jointz has background vocals on the word "clear". It's unknown if this version was for DONDA or JIIK, but it's likely it was meant for JIIK as the song includes a "Hands On" bridge. Kanye's part of the song is probably compressed a lot rougher than how it was supposed to be bounced, as Dre's verse isn't as distorted. Unknown when this was made, but was found in Steve Lacy's Dropbox, who only worked with Kanye after June 2021. Samples Robert G. Moore Jr "Preacher Goes Off", as well as the 2019 Big Boy interview.

(feat. Ant Clemons & Young Thug) (prod. BONGO ByTheWay)

Name:
Welcome To UCLA [V2]
(prod. Timbaland)
(Jam 6, UCLA)
Notes:
OG Filename: Welcome To UCLA (1st Rough arrangement)
Very unfinished track. Made during the Timbaland sessions in December 2018. It contains one of the sounds Timbaland used in "Jam 7" - sampling "Arcade Echo FX 001".

OG Filename: NO ID - BIG SEAN - KW - ALL THE TIME _rkROUGH 4.18.13
2013 version with verses from Big Sean. Possibly intended for his Good Music Chicago project, but the filename leaves the ownership of the song unclear.

(Don't Let Me Down, Hurricanes, 80 Degrees)
Notes:
OG Filename: Hurricane [Francis and Cashmere KW vrs] 10.06.18
Completely different beat with Kanye's verses vocoded. Sold and leaked as "V2". First previewed in the October 2018 Virgil Abloh promo Kanye put on his Twitter. Leaked in mono.

(Freestyle 10)

(feat. Sunday Service Choir)
(Chakras, The Chakra, Chakra, I Love It (Remix), Good Morning)
Notes: Version that uses the censored Yandhi-era vocal take on the JESUS IS KING-era instrumental. The "yeezy" chant appears earlier and is more pronounced than the final version. Played at the Detroit listening party and the IMAX movie.

(Don't Let Me Down, Hurricanes, 80 Degrees)

OG Filename: Hurricane 09.23.18 [New KW Vrs]
Early Ant Clemons hook with one pretty much finished Kanye verse and an open verse.

Name:
??? [V2]
(prod. Charlie Heat)
(You Know It)
Notes: Snippet played in a documentary about Charlie Heat (snippet at 2:11). Nothing is known apart from the fact that it was made sometime in 2015 and has Charlie Heat production. Was put as a grail for some reason by previous editors.

Name:

Notes: Very similar to the previous version, but uses more of the sample chop, has a different vocal take for the first chorus, and a new (mumble) 2nd verse. Leaked with stems after a groupbuy.

(prod. 88-Keys, Hudson Mohawke, MIKE DEAN & Noah Goldstein)
(Do You Really Ever Love)
Notes:
OG Filename: KW - God Level Ref (5.22.14)
Version of the song which features three different beats. Shares a sample with "Pressure". An edited version of the track was officially used in an Adidas commercial in 2014. The chop at the end was separated into a July 2014 file later used in August 2015.

Travis Scott - Piss On Your Grave [V11]

Notes:

Notes:

Filename seen from a picture of Post Malone refs.

(Bad Taste, Use This Gospel, Breastplate Of Right, Grab Your Armour For Protection, Breastplate Of Righteousness, You Don't Know)

Fully finished Yandhi-era song. Sold and leaked along with the stems, incorrectly under the name "Bye Bye Baby." Possibly the same song as "Spread My Wings" from 2020, but unconfirmed.

Name:
Piss On Your Grave [V1]
(prod. Kanye West)
Notes: Original "Piss On Your Grave" freestyle, recorded over the base sample (bleed can be heard in Kanye's vocal stem). Contains extra vocals as shown in the 88-Keys snippet.

Name:
Piss On Your Grave [V4]
(prod. Kanye West & Charlie Heat)
Notes: Solo version with mumble bars before it was given to Travis in mid 2015. Shown to label execs in December 2014 or January 2015 as a SHMG album track. Stems for this version are dated as being from October 2nd, 2014.

Travis Scott - Piss On Your Grave [V8]

(feat. Charlie Wilson) (prod. 7 Aurelius, Irv Gotti & Bink!)

Name:
🗑️ Saint Pablo [V12]
(feat. Sampha) (prod. Keyon Christ)
Notes: Uses an altered version of "4 4 4 4" in the instrumental. Features some alternate lyrics from Sampha in the final verse. Likely made in January in Keyon's second session with Kanye while he worked on "FML" & "Face Down". Was rumored to be the version for Turbo Grafx 16, but Keyon Christ has deconfirmed this. He has also stated that he "hates this one".

Name:
Pusha T - Santeria [V4]
(feat. Kanye West) (prod. Kanye West)

(Internet, Instalove)
Notes: Version with an unfinished Kanye verse and hook. His vocals possibly came from the 2014 Madonna sessions, where BloodPop® was working with Dahi. A version without Kanye's vocals and production leaked in 2018, and was released on Post Malone's 2019 album, Hollywood's Bleeding.

Kanye's vocals are still unfinished in this version. Big Sean and Quavo are now featured. Kanye drops the F slur multiple times. Likely recorded during Cruel Winter/Turbo Grafx 16 sessions.

(Euro 2, Switch Hands)

OG Filename: EURO2_Motivated - Asap (Switch Hands)
Has a different song structure. Portions of the track don't feature vocals with minor effects differences. Song was given to Rocky, proven by the original filename. Unedited file leaked in December 2022.

Notes: Demo version of the G.O.O.D. Music remix that features Sia vocals. Features unfilled open verses, likely for Desiigner per V2.

Name:
Ty Dolla $ign - No Panties
(prod. Mustard & DJ Dahi)
(I Don't Fuck With You, Turn Up)
Notes: Ty Dolla $ign song that uses the same beat that was given to Big Sean for "I Don't Fuck With You". It is unknown if this was made before or after Justin Bieber's version.

✨ Shoot Up The Station [V1]
(feat. Bon Iver & Santigold) (prod. Kutta)
Notes: Unfinished track believed to be Good Ass Job era, as Kanye worked with Bon Iver in 2009-2010 ("Monster" / "Lost in the World"), and Kanye worked with Santigold in 2009. ("Gifted") Beat was used officially in 2011 for B.o.B's "Shoot Up The Station".

Name:
Too Excited [V8]
(prod. Timbaland)
(Me Too, Jam 14)
Notes: Recording snippet from the Timbaland session. Said by Alek to be an earlier version than the one that later leaked in full, as it has a completely different melody compared to the other versions we know of, however the video of Timbaland previewing this video was posted in March of 2019, so it could also be later.

Name:
I Miss The Old Kanye [V1]
(ref. Malik Yusef)
(I Love Kanye)

1:50 longer than the later version and has a longer, more mumbled verse from Post Malone, along with Kanye's full mumble verse. Leaked fully in early November 2018 by iTagen.

(Lap Dance, My Ex Best Friend)

OG Filename: Travis Scott - 411
Throwaway from Rodeo featuring James Fauntleroy. Seen on a tracklist for the album crossed out. Snippet was posted on Travis's Snapchat story on June 13th, 2015, and then leaked in full on November 2nd, 2018. Was sold by Music Mafia.

Name:
✨ Rich Nigga Drunk [V5]
(feat. Tyga)
Notes:
OG Filename: Rich Nigga Drunk Ruff- BOUNCE
Version with a Tyga feature. His verse is a feature, rather than reference vocals, like on "Bad Night" and "Only".

(ref. Tyga)
(Make U Luv Me)
Notes:
OG Filename: Only Ye Reference-TYGA
Song was thought to be titled "Only Ye", but it's actually a reference for "Only", as Tyga raps a verse intended for Kanye. Most likely came from the same studio session as "Rich Nigga Drunk" and "Bad Night". This is a more finished version compared to the other solo snippet of this song that leaked from Music Mafia.

Name:
6ix9ine - MAMA [V1]
(prod. Sool Got Hits & Murda Beatz)
Notes: Includes the original line "Splish, splash, Fashion Nova make that ass fat" that 6ix9ine later cut. Fashion Nova paid six figures for the line, but Nicki Minaj asked for it to be cut because of her beef with Cardi B. This version has no Kanye or Nicki Minaj vocals.

Name:
Bad Night [V11]
(ref. Tyga) (feat. Young Thug)
(Rap Tarantino, Man Up, Too Reckless, Bad Guy)
Notes:
OG Filename: Bad Night-Tyga Ref BOUNCE
Tyga reference track.

Name:
???
(Good Job)
Notes: Recording of Chance and Kanye together, likely recording references for Good Ass Job. Title is unknown, but "Good Job" is heard numerous times throughout the snippet. Was posted in mid-September 2018.

Name:
Don't Let Me Down [V6] 
(feat. G Herbo) (prod. BoogzDaBeast, Nascent & RONNY J)
(Hurricane, Hurricanes, 80 Degrees)
Notes: Version with a G Herbo feature. Snippet posted to Kanye's Instagram in the studio with him. Confirmed by the "Hurricane" Pro Tools Session.

Name:
Don't Let Me Down [V2]
(prod. BoogzDaBeast, Nascent & RONNY J)
(Hurricane, Hurricanes, 80 Degrees)
Notes: The beat from "Hurricane" was originally for Good Ass Job (2018), but when Chance didn't want it, Kanye sang over it as the first track for his new album that would become Yandhi. This version is the one used in the iconic spinning CD video Kanye posted to his social media promoting the release of Yandhi.

Name:
⭐ Post Malone - No Reason [V3]
(feat. Kanye West & Justin Bieber) (prod. Charlie Heat)
(100 Years Now)
Notes: Later version that doesn't include Kanye's mumble verse, cuts down Post Malone's verse, and features alternate production. Most likely Post's song at this point, as Kanye's vocals are only on the hook and Post's verse is edited to have less mumble.

Name:
XTCY [V13]
(prod. Kanye West & Andrew Dawson)
(Extacty, Extacy)
Notes: Original WeTransfer version posted by Kanye to Twitter. Same as the streaming release and contains the OG metadata, but is unmastered.

Name:
Teyana Taylor - We Got Love [V2]
(prod. Kanye West)
Notes: Version played at the K.T.S.E. listening part. Was scrapped from the album last second due of sample clearance issues.

Name:
XXXTENTACION - One Minute [V1]
(prod. John Cunningham)
Notes: Original version previewed four days before XXXTENTACION died. Did not feature Kanye at the time as the two never worked together while X was alive. Any other differences are currently unknown.

Name:
Teyana Taylor - Rose In Harlem [V2]
(prod. Kanye West, BoogzDaBeast, E.VAX, johan lenox & MIKE DEAN)
Notes: Version previewed on Teyana's Instagram. Has a different outro to release, some lyrics differ, and the instrumental has more bass.

Name:
Freeee (Ghost Town Pt. 2) [V13]
(feat. Ty Dolla $ign) (prod. Kanye West, Kid Cudi, Andrew Dawson, Andy C, BoogzDaBeast, MIKE DEAN, Jeff Bhasker & Russel "Love" Crews)
(Free, Freeee, I Feel Free)
Notes: Version with a spliced in Cudi punch-in 54 seconds in. Was played at the listening party.

Name:
Cudi Montage [V4]
(feat. Mr Hudson) (prod. Kid Cudi, Dot Da Genius & MIKE DEAN)
Notes: Early version with an alternate vocal take for Kanye's verse. A stem edit can be found in Fakes that was thought to be the full file, however you could clearly tell it was spliced with a spectrogram.

Name:
XXXTENTACION - Yes Indeed (Freestyle)
(prod. Wheezy & B-Rackz)
Notes: The XXXTENTACION verse used on "The Storm" is from a freestyle over the "Yes Indeed" beat before he died. After X was removed from "The Storm", the vocals were released on "Voices" by Skye.

Name:
FML [V14]
(feat. The Weeknd) (prod. Metro Boomin)
(FMLU)
Notes: Further along version with no Travis Scott vocals and reused instrumental parts from the SWISH versions of the song. Slightly tagged version was leaked by Music Mafia May 1st, 2018.

Name:
Don't Play With That Boy [V1]
(prod. Kanye West)
(Ye vs. the People)
Notes: Solo LOVE EVERYONE scrap with a beat later reused for "Ye vs. the People", but is otherwise an entirely different song. Snippet was played in the behind the scenes "kanye west / t.i." video posted to Kanye's YouTube channel.

Name:
Ye vs. the People [V2]
(starring T.I. as the People) (prod. Kanye West)
(Don't Play With That Boy)
Notes: Version previewed at the end of the Kanye and T.I. video. Has a single line change in the snippet.

Name:
??? [V1]
(prod. Kanye West)
(Amistad, Rich Nigga Shit)
Notes:
Lyrics were found in the LOVE EVERYONE lyrics notepad, and it was performed for TMZ on April 26th, 2018. Samples "Idea #36" by Raymond Scott. The chop was eventually reused and developed further for the Young Thug song "Rich Nigga Shit" from his 2021 album Punk. Lyrics:

"Parents are the strippers
Strip kids of they confidence
Teach white dominance
Question your common sense
I been washed in tradition that ima rence
I hopped off the amistad and made I'm a god. Oh it's gone be a litigation any time I'm involved
Oh it's gone be some litigation if it's not resolved"

(with 070 Shake) (prod. Keyon Christ & Metro Boomin)
Notes: Solo 070 Shake version. Likely recorded by her during Cruel Winter sessions.

Name:
Madlib Freestyle 6
(feat. Kendrick Lamar) (prod. Madlib)
Notes: High energy freestyle with Kendrick Lamar. Rumoured to be part of the "40 tracks" they recorded together during The Life of Pablo-era.

Name:
Real Friends (Remix)
(feat. DMX & Ty Dolla $ign) (prod. Kanye West, Darren King, Boi-1da, Frank Dukes, Havoc & Noah Goldstein)
Notes: Official remix with a verse from DMX. Was announced by Swizz Beatz in 2016 on Instagram, but never released.

Name:
A New State of Mind
(with State of Mind) (prod. Kanye West)
Notes: Demo made by the first group Kanye was in called State Of Mind, formed by him and his friend Eugene "Frexx" McNair Jr. Recorded in the early 1990s, and was on a 1993 State of Mind demo tape. Snippet was played in the Channel 4 documentary Public Enemies: JAY-Z vs Kanye.

Name:
⭐ Hold Tight [V3]
(feat. Migos & Young Thug) (prod. Charlie Heat)
Notes: Snippets originally previewed by Young Thug and Migos shortly after the release of The Life Of Pablo, possibly making it one of the first songs recorded for Turbo Grafx 16. Formerly known as "Black Tron".

Name:
Pressure [V8]
(prod. Havoc)
(CAN U BE, Never Let Me Go)
Notes: Version previously owned by MusicMafia, who attempted a failed groupbuy for the song priced at 2 BTC in 2017. This version of the song was likely made after the leaked version Travis Scott recorded over as it has a structure change, less mumble lines and a more on-beat bass stem (the leaked one is ~41ms off-beat). Original snippet leaked June 4th, 2017. Two new snippets leaked May 22nd and May 23rd, 2024, after an edited version with AI Travis Scott vocals was played live by Kick streamer Adin Ross, who bought the song for 200k in November 2023. Fourth snippet leaked May 25th, 2024, following a 100k groupbuy offer. Adin Ross later played his version in full on stream on August 10th, 2024.

Name:
Motivated [V1]
(prod. TM88)
(Euro 2, Switch Hands)
Notes: Solo Kanye version, with an open verse. Leaked after producer TM88 got hacked.

Name:

(feat. Kanye West) (prod. Tyler, The Creator)
Notes: Early version described as Kanye "speaking pure nonsense" with "insane and ridiculous lyrics, making it unreleasable", so likely mumble, alongside alternate Tyler lyrics. Tyler's verse is also rumored to be Lil Wayne's verse.

(ref. Consequence) (prod. Benji B & Kanye West)

OG Filename: KW - Good Things Don't Last Ref (3.10.13)

Scrapped demo with Kanye humming and singing along under heavy vocoding, similar to "Runaway". Prominently samples "Dedicated to the One I Love" by The Mamas and the Papas.

Name: ✨ New Angels [V1]
Notes:
OG Filename: KW - New Angels Ref (1.7.14)
Yeezus 2 era throwaway. First 46 seconds have no mumble, apart from that it has singing with some mumble. Lossless unedited file leaked in 2022.

Name:
Wolves [V12]
(feat. VIC MENSA, Sia & Frank Ocean) (prod. Cashmere Cat & Sinjin Hawke)
(Lost)
Notes:
OG Filename: Wolves Ref (2.24.15) CASHMERE CAT
Release ready version of the song. Sia has one less line than on previous versions. Meant for the now scrapped A Side / B Side single release of "All Day" & "Wolves", which was meant to release on February 24th, 2015 based on his iTunes Artist Page updating, Joe Perez's single art, MIKE DEAN hyping it up on Twitter and Benji B being confused by it's delay on Twitter. Was delayed by a week for unknown reasons and the next release day, (March 3rd, 2015) "All Day" released on it's own without "Wolves". Song was purchased from MUSICMAFIA by a Section80 user and eventually leaked in the February 2016 leak season.

Name:
Wolves [V13]
(feat. VIC MENSA) (prod. Cashmere Cat & Sinjin Hawke)
(Lost)
Notes:
OG Filename: KW - Wolves XOYO Ref (3.3.15)
Version played at XOYO. Does not have Sia's verse.

Name:
The Mind Is Powerful [V1]
(feat. Tricky Stewart) (prod. 88-Keys)
(Mind Power, Start Time, Star Time)
Notes: First known version of "The Mind Is Powerful" / "Star Time" containing freestyle verses from Kanye and chorus vocals from Tricky Stewart (The-Dream's writing partner). Likely dated either February 2nd or February 3rd as both Kanye and Tricky Stewart recorded their vocals on the 2nd, with The-Dream recording for the song on the 3rd. Samples "Jungle" by James Brown, and a live performance of "Mary Jane" by Rick James.

First version with The Weeknd. He replaces part of Kanye's mumble hook. Has new drums and extended sample outro.

(Enya)
Notes: Version that includes part of the "Return To Innocence" sample, as well as the redone production by Hudson Mohawke. Still contains a mumble second Kanye verse. Cuts off abruptly, may be a bounce issue. Even though the song cuts before Cudi's vocals would appear in the song, this version seems to be from after he was added to the song.

Likely a partial file due to the fade out at the end, which seemingly only cuts out a short instrumental portion.

(Go Pro)

Has a similar beat to the first SWISH version but with more drums, a Madonna feature and Mez reference vocals. The first verse, while short, is finished and partially reused on the finale version, while the second verse is complete mumble and seemingly wasn't revisited on later versions. Has Mez's reference vocals layered over Kanye's mumble verse.

Part of Drake's verse is used in later versions, with an extra line not found in this one. Unclear if this was a version edited specifically for OVO Radio, but considering the verse is from Kanye versions of the song and the beat is the less developed version used in other Kanye versions, it's likely this is the same initial recording Drake did for the song in 2014.

Name:
✨ See Me Again [V1]
(See You Again, See Me, Never See Me Again)
Notes: Unfinished track with some mumble lyrics, leaked just over a month before My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy dropped. Was leaked as "Never See Me Again", but current leakers who have the song confirm that the title is "See Me Again". Interpolates "I Never Want To See You Again" by Quasi. This leak gained notable popularity after a widespread theory that it was a "suicide note" of sorts, further influenced by the discovery that the song shared a similar chord progression to "Futari Dake No CEREMONY" by the late J-pop star Yukiko Okada, who comitted suicide in April of 1986 due to alleged relationship issues - which can allude to Kanye's troubles at this time that would result in his breakup with Amber Rose - however this relation has since been debunked and is mostly viewed as a coincidence.

The song did end up on Teairra's Don't Make Me Cause A Scene mixtape, however the version on that mixtape was shorter than the leak.

Could have been recorded in either 2005 or 2006, but was most likely after Late Registration's release. Kanye later took this song and added another verse for The Graduate mixtape he co-hosted and dropped on his website. Samples "Blues for Salvador" by Santana.
